title: Day 99 - OOP Classes Practice
description: Implement real-world classes in C++ with proper methods and edge case handling.

steps.title: Setup Customer Class
steps.instructions: Create a class Customer with attributes: name (string), accountNumber (int), accountBalance (double), isActive (bool). Also create a constructor.

steps.title: Implement Deposit
steps.instructions: Add a method deposit(double amount) that increases accountBalance.

steps.title: Implement Withdraw
steps.instructions: Add withdraw(double amount). Prevent withdrawal if balance is insufficient.

steps.title: Implement Transfer
steps.instructions: Create transfer(double amount, Customer &target). Deduct from sender and add to receiver.

steps.title: Print Customer Details
steps.instructions: Create printDetails() to display name, account number and balance.

steps.title: Test Customer Class
steps.instructions: Create 2 Customer objects in main() and perform deposit, withdraw and transfer.
hints: Test edge cases like low balance

steps.title: Setup Car Class
steps.instructions: Create class Car with model, year, fuelLevel, speed, isRunning.

steps.title: Acceleration Logic
steps.instructions: Implement accelerate(int amount). Increase speed only if engine is ON and fuel > 0.

steps.title: Brake Logic
steps.instructions: Implement brake(int amount). Ensure speed does not go below 0.

steps.title: Refuel
steps.instructions: Add refuel(double amount). Cap fuelLevel at 100.

steps.title: Print Car Status
steps.instructions: Display model, speed, fuelLevel and running state.

steps.title: Setup Laptop Class
steps.instructions: Create class Laptop with brand, screenSize, processor, ram, storage, batteryLevel, isOn.

steps.title: Open Apps
steps.instructions: Implement openApps(int numApps). Reduce battery by numApps * 2.

steps.title: Close Apps
steps.instructions: Implement closeApps(int numApps). Increase battery slightly.

steps.title: Charging Logic
steps.instructions: Implement charge(double amount). Cap battery at 100.

steps.title: Final Integration
steps.instructions: In main(), create objects of all three classes and simulate real-world operations. Show outputs clearly.
hints: Do at least 3 operations per class
